name: Young's Fish Market
tagline: Old-school Hawaiian plate lunch counter in Kalihi — massive portions, family recipes, and some of the best poke on the island.
mustOrder: Hawaiian plate — laulau, kalua pig, lomi salmon, poi or rice
Poke by the pound — ahi, tako, or shoyu varieties
parking: Small lot on Liliha St. Street parking on side streets
bestTime: Lunch is peak — go before 11am or after 1:30pm to skip the line
talkStory: Young's has been serving Hawaiian food in Kalihi since 1951, making it one of the oldest continuously operating Hawaiian food spots on Oahu. Three generations of the Young family have run it. The Hawaiian plate — laulau, kalua pig, lomi salmon, poi — is a complete meal and a cultural education. Their poke counter is among the island's best, with fresh ahi and tako sold by the pound. The pipikaula (Hawaiian dried beef) is hard to find anywhere else. Not fancy — order at the counter, eat at plastic tables. This is real local food.
